name: Lone John Sliver
recipeInstructions:
THE BATTER: In a 1 1/2 quart mixing bowl, with wire whisk, whip 2/3 cup beer, lemon juice, egg, Bisquick, and 1/2 cup self-rising flour together until smooth. Let it stand ten minutes at room temperature before using. Meanwhile, place two soup or cereal bowls on the counter near the stove so that you can work quickly in coating food and getting it into the hot oil.
In one small bowl, put 1/2 cup beer. In another small bowl, put 1/2 cup self-rising flour. Have a cookie sheet on hand. Cover half of it with paper towels for draining the fried foods. The other half should be left plain for drying the coated food before dipping into batter.
Allow 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 lbs. fish fillets to serve 4 people. Cut each fillet in half to resemble triangles. Dip one piece at a time, first into beer. Let excess drip off. Coat lightly with self-rising flour.
Place floured pieces on the cookie sheet to dry for 2 or 3 minutes. Then spear the piece with a fork or tip of a sharp knife and coat evenly but lightly in the prepared batter, letting excess drip back into the bowl of batter.
Fry a few pieces at a time in oil heated to 385 to 400 degrees F (least 2 1/2 inches deep) in a heavy saucepan or electric fry pan with temperature control dial. Allow about 3 or 4 minutes per side for each piece of batter-coated fish, turning once to brown the other side.
Remove from oil with tip of a sharp knife. Drain on paper towels. Keep warm on the cookie sheet in a 300 degree F oven until all pieces have been fried.
SHRIMP-DEEP FRIED: Substitute large or jumbo shrimp, preferably frozen and cooked, ready-to-serve shrimp that have been completely thawed, following the Lone John Sliver recipe exactly, using the shrimp in place of the fish triangles. Be sure to dip the shrimp first into beer, then into self-rising flour before coating in prepared batter. Fry as directed for fish in the above recipe. 1 pound serves 4.
